整套首页
unit_10 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_10

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 314 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC TRIPLE
Ultimately, the young idealists (i)__________ the society they so fervently desired to reform: in their frustration, they reacted to the immediate situation by removing themselves from the postwar social and political arena, where the future of their country was being decided. In doing so, they (ii)_________ the salutary effects their rebellion should have (iii)____________ .Ultimately, the young idealists (i)__________ the society they so fervently desired to reform: in their frustration, they reacted to the immediate situation by removing themselves from the postwar social and political arena, where the future of their country was being decided. In doing so, they (ii)_________ the salutary effects their rebellion should have (iii)____________ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　did a great disservice to; nullified; begotten
salutarytributesubvert
salutary: standard unit headword | tribute: standard unit headword | subvert: standard unit headword
#2 Text Completion
Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.Environmental models—mathematical representations designed to simulate natural systems—are regularly used by litigants in legal disputes over environmental issues. Unfortunately, the (i)______ scientific model is (ii)______ in environmental tort litigation. Because of the adversarial nature of litigation, models are often used by one side to (iii)______ empirical evidence presented by the other. And because modeling is a particularly technical field, the task of assessing a given model’s relevance and reliability may exceed the abilities of judges and juries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　uncertainty inherent in; exacerbated; obfuscate
obfuscateadducenaturedesign
obfuscate: standard unit headword | adduce: standard unit headword | nature: standard unit headword | design: standard unit headword
#3 Sentence Equivalence
Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the ______ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.Edited collections of scholarly essays generally tend to be somewhat uneven: they suffer from the ______ subject matter of the various essays, the lack of an overarching and consistent thesis, and the variable quantity of the contributions.
答案：B, C　disparate; heterogeneous
subjectsubject matter
subject: standard unit headword | subject matter: standard unit headword
#4 TC SINGLE
The efficacy of a placebo may not ___________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.The efficacy of a placebo may not ___________ deception: inert sugar pills have been shown to reduce the symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome even in patients who were explicitly told they were receiving a placebo.
答案：C　require
placeboinert
placebo: standard unit headword | inert: standard unit headword
#5 Text Completion
Needing an advocate who would be both precise and succinct, they rejected McLintock, whose inveterate ______ would automatically preclude meeting those requirements.Needing an advocate who would be both precise and succinct, they rejected McLintock, whose inveterate ______ would automatically preclude meeting those requirements.
答案：E　prolixity
succinctmeet
succinct: standard unit headword | meet: standard unit headword
#6 Sentence Equivalence
While recognizing that recent reports of cyberwarfare, phone-hacking scandals, and identity thefts have tended to accent the destructive connotation of the word, Sue Halpern maintains that “hacking” is such ______ term that its meaning nearly always derives from its context.While recognizing that recent reports of cyberwarfare, phone-hacking scandals, and identity thefts have tended to accent the destructive connotation of the word, Sue Halpern maintains that “hacking” is such ______ term that its meaning nearly always derives from its context.
答案：A, B　a generic; an inclusive
genericaffirmative
generic: standard unit headword | affirmative: standard unit headword
#7 TC TRIPLE
Since scientific truths must be discovered, and since many, probably most, are far from (i) __________, futile investigations are (ii) __________. Thus, the path to the truth is decidedly a (iii) __________ one.Since scientific truths must be discovered, and since many, probably most, are far from (i) __________, futile investigations are (ii) __________. Thus, the path to the truth is decidedly a (iii) __________ one.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　intuitively obvious; inevitable; sinuous
far fromauspicious
far from: standard unit headword | auspicious: standard unit headword
#8 Text Completion
Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)______. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)______. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　competitive; wide-ranging; comprehensive
tediouskeen
tedious: standard unit headword | keen: standard unit headword
#9 Sentence Equivalence
It is commonly said that each person’s aesthetic taste is ______, yet that notion overlooks a large body of shared aesthetic preferences.It is commonly said that each person’s aesthetic taste is ______, yet that notion overlooks a large body of shared aesthetic preferences.
答案：C, E　distinct; idiosyncratic
idiosyncraticacquired
idiosyncratic: standard unit headword | acquired: standard unit headword
#10 Sentence Equivalence
Progressive and reactionary populist movements are not necessarily ______: each may, and usually does, possess the features of the other.Progressive and reactionary populist movements are not necessarily ______: each may, and usually does, possess the features of the other.
答案：A, D　dichotomous; contradictory
subversivepopulist
subversive: standard unit headword | populist: standard unit headword
#11 Sentence Equivalence
Compared to their predecessors, who were more nationalist than feminist in political orientation, Roman women activists of the younger generation are more inclined to ______ women’s rights, often creating organizations focused primarily on women’s issues.Compared to their predecessors, who were more nationalist than feminist in political orientation, Roman women activists of the younger generation are more inclined to ______ women’s rights, often creating organizations focused primarily on women’s issues.
答案：C, F　champion; defend
feministdownplay
feminist: standard unit headword | downplay: standard unit headword

By cultivating instinctive, irrational awarenesses--which might come from dreams or from accepting results derived by chance or from a receptivity to taboo--they hoped to (iii) _________ their audience's sense of everyday reality.The artists sought to (i) _________ precisely the impulses that traditional or generally accepted thinking had (ii) ________. By cultivating instinctive, irrational awarenesses--which might come from dreams or from accepting results derived by chance or from a receptivity to taboo--they hoped to (iii) _________ their audience's sense of everyday reality.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　affirm; marginalized; unsettle

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　esoteric; parochial

The biographer laments the________ that abound in texts from antiquity; they are so prevalent, she says, that they nearly amount to a “conspiracy of silence.”The biographer laments the________ that abound in texts from antiquity; they are so prevalent, she says, that they nearly amount to a “conspiracy of silence.”
答案：D, E　elisions; lacunae
lament
lament: standard unit headword

The poet produced a body of work that was unfailingly tender yet never __________, always stopping short of outright sentimentality.The poet produced a body of work that was unfailingly tender yet never __________, always stopping short of outright sentimentality.
答案：C　mawkish
outright
outright: standard unit headword
#16 SE
Despite all the efforts of his admirers to bolster his prestige, the reputation of Alexander Pope, both as a person and as a writer, remains __________.Despite all the efforts of his admirers to bolster his prestige, the reputation of Alexander Pope, both as a person and as a writer, remains __________.
答案：A, E　insecure; precarious
precarious
precarious: standard unit headword
#17 TC DOUBLE
Faced with a drastic loss of revenue, executives of local newspapers belong to two strategic camps: the (i)_______ and the (ii)_______. The former, trying to do more of the same but more cheaply, are doomed. Only the latter, embracing a fundamentally different way of doing business, will prosper in the face of unstoppable change.Faced with a drastic loss of revenue, executives of local newspapers belong to two strategic camps: the (i)_______ and the (ii)_______. The former, trying to do more of the same but more cheaply, are doomed. Only the latter, embracing a fundamentally different way of doing business, will prosper in the face of unstoppable change.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　economizers; visionaries
drastic
drastic: standard unit headword
#18 SE

答案：B, E　oppositionality; protest

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　not entirely surprised; discourage

Blank 2

Needing to conceal their increasing alienation from each other, the family settled into what amounted to voluble nonsense, in that they would ____________ but seldom communicate.Needing to conceal their increasing alienation from each other, the family settled into what amounted to voluble nonsense, in that they would ____________ but seldom communicate.
答案：C　natter

To a mathematician, it means little that the equations (iii)_______. They want proof that the equations are unfailing: that no matter the fluid, and no matter how far into the future you forecast its flow, the mathematics of the equations will still hold.While physicists consider the Navier-Stokes equations used to model the movement of fluids to be (i)_______, mathematicians are (ii)_______ them. To a mathematician, it means little that the equations (iii)_______. They want proof that the equations are unfailing: that no matter the fluid, and no matter how far into the future you forecast its flow, the mathematics of the equations will still hold.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　reliable; skeptical regarding; appear to work

答案：B, C　an incipient; a nascent

In truth, the preservation of the reef (iii)______, a combination of active government intervention and the beneficial effects of responsible tourism.

Blank 3

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　losing interest in; spurious; quality

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　an adherence to; tame; embraces

答案：D　unqualified

As human interactions become (ii)______ physical location, people are less likely to have regular dealings with others who do not share the same values and outlooks.

Blank 2

Blank 3

A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)______. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)______, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.A certain amount of theoretical frenzies about comics today is (i)______. After all, similar frenzies have been seen in other art forms in the process of their rapid development, for instance, the debating about the paintings that roiled Renaissance Italy. But such intellectual (ii)______ rarely preceded creative glory. On the contrary, it indicates that an artistic (iii)______, having been made and recognized, is over, and that a process of increasingly strained emulation and diminishing returns is set in.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　understandable; arousal; breakthrough

答案：D, F　lull; respite

#55 Text Completion
Despite the neighborhood’s lingering reputation for ______, it has in fact become increasingly varied in its architecture and demographics.Despite the neighborhood’s lingering reputation for ______, it has in fact become increasingly varied in its architecture and demographics.
答案：E　homogeneity
lingering
lingering: standard unit headword
#56 Text Completion
The journalist was someone whose habitual distrust of authority struck a few people as (i)______ but who had enough talent and charm that most found the trait to be (ii)______, with the result that it did not become a personal or professional liability.The journalist was someone whose habitual distrust of authority struck a few people as (i)______ but who had enough talent and charm that most found the trait to be (ii)______, with the result that it did not become a personal or professional liability.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　pathological; pardonable

答案：B, F　familiar; recognizable
forbidding
forbidding: standard unit headword
#58 Sentence Equivalence
For wily political press officers, the art of spin is not quite supplanting truth with lies, but instead it aspires to replace awkward complexity with catchy ______; successful spin creates the impression of unavoidable common sense.For wily political press officers, the art of spin is not quite supplanting truth with lies, but instead it aspires to replace awkward complexity with catchy ______; successful spin creates the impression of unavoidable common sense.
答案：E, F　simplicity; intelligibility
falsehood
falsehood: standard unit headword

Having displayed his art collection in a vast modernist white space in (i)______ former warehouse, Mr. Saatchi has chosen for his new site its polar opposite, a riverside monument to civic pomposity that once housed the local government. There is nothing (ii)______ about the new location: the building’s design is bureaucratic baroque, (iii)______ style that is as declamatory as a task-force report and as self-regarding as a campaign speech.Having displayed his art collection in a vast modernist white space in (i)______ former warehouse, Mr. Saatchi has chosen for his new site its polar opposite, a riverside monument to civic pomposity that once housed the local government. There is nothing (ii)______ about the new location: the building’s design is bureaucratic baroque, (iii)______ style that is as declamatory as a task-force report and as self-regarding as a campaign speech.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　an unprepossessing; spare; a grandiose

Physicians today increasingly rely on endoscopic surgery, replacing large scalpels and clamps with cameras and with tools whose ______ enables them to snake into the body through tiny holes.Physicians today increasingly rely on endoscopic surgery, replacing large scalpels and clamps with cameras and with tools whose ______ enables them to snake into the body through tiny holes.
答案：A, D　flexibility; suppleness

The controversy about Alexander the Great’s personality derives from the fact that our sources are (i)______: all eyewitness accounts having perished. What remains is, at best, (ii)______ (one history, for instance, is based largely on the now-lost memoirs of Alexander’s alleged half-brother, Ptolemy) and at worst, highly unreliable.The controversy about Alexander the Great’s personality derives from the fact that our sources are (i)______: all eyewitness accounts having perished. What remains is, at best, (ii)______ (one history, for instance, is based largely on the now-lost memoirs of Alexander’s alleged half-brother, Ptolemy) and at worst, highly unreliable.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　inadequate; secondhand

#68 Text Completion
For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)______: the witness to that is the (ii)______ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.For a time in the early Middle Ages, Latin culture came close to (i)______: the witness to that is the (ii)______ of manuscript copies of texts datable to the period. The process of copying manuscripts, the only way in which the fragile products of centuries of accumulating knowledge could be preserved, (iii)______, a situation that did not change for two and a half centuries, until the time of Charlemagne. In the intervening period, much of Classical literature was lost to us forever.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　vanishing; paucity; virtually came to a standstill

#69 TC TRIPLE
Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) ____________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) ____________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) ____________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.Singers of the tropicalist movement in Brazil goaded the government by purposefully invoking stereotypical images of Brazil as a tropical paradise only to (i) ____________ them with pointed references to political and social problems. But (ii) ____________ in giving offense, the tropicalist singers also managed to annoy the Brazilian left, which shared their social concerns. They embraced an irreverent politics and (iii) ____________, epitomized by the coexistence of bossa nova and rock in the same song, that ignored certain leftist calls for folkloric authenticity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　subvert; ecumenical; a pastiche of styles

subvert: standard unit headword

As is often the case, however, theoretical commitments are (ii)______ actual practices. In reality, gender-based (iii)______ persists.Traditional Vietnamese culture has long promoted the idea of gender equality. Founding myths (i)______ the equal division of labor in child care for mothers and fathers. As is often the case, however, theoretical commitments are (ii)______ actual practices. In reality, gender-based (iii)______ persists.

Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　celebrate; incommensurate with; stratification

#71 Text Completion
Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)______ . The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)______ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)______ , Critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.Vaccine denial has all the hallmarks of a belief system that is not (i)______ . The notion that childhood vaccines are driving autism rates has been (ii)______ by multiple epidemiological studies. Yet the true believers are (iii)______ , Critiquing each new study that challenges their views, and rallying to the defense of disgraced researchers whose work was retracted.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　amenable to refutation; upended; persistent

#72 TC TRIPLE
In 1987, Japanese chemists demonstrated that tiny particles of gold could act as (i)________ facilitating the combination of carbon monoxide and oxygen into carbon dioxide. The idea that gold could hasten chemical reactions was (ii)________: after all, gold is (iii)________ in its bulk form. It has been used for millennia to make jewelry precisely because it does not tarnish by reacting with oxygen or water.In 1987, Japanese chemists demonstrated that tiny particles of gold could act as (i)________ facilitating the combination of carbon monoxide and oxygen into carbon dioxide. The idea that gold could hasten chemical reactions was (ii)________: after all, gold is (iii)________ in its bulk form. It has been used for millennia to make jewelry precisely because it does not tarnish by reacting with oxygen or water.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　a catalyst; counterintuitive; inert

Some hold the view that North America, Asia, and South America had (ii)______ immediately following the dinosaur extinction, pointing to (iii)______ between ancient kinds of mammals that existed on all three continents at this time in support of their argument.Not all paleontologists agree that connections between the continents were (i)______ just after the extinction of the dinosaurs. #75 Text Completion
For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i)______ . Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii)______ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii)______ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry’s bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.For Ruskin, architecture serves the community only when approached in a spirit of piety and (i)______ . Architecture must set effective boundaries to public space, and it does so by (ii)______ the desire to show off, to stand out, to record the artistic flair of some temporary ego. Architecture succeeds in its public task through (iii)______ and devotion, of the kind that can be observed in the moulding, firing and laying of a properly proportioned brick, but which is violated at every point by Frank Gehry’s bombastic Guggenheim Museum in Bilbao.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　sacrifice; relinquishing; humility

#76 TC TRIPLE
The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　More than; affirmative; recovery
affirmative
affirmative: standard unit headword

Fables often endure due to their (i)______ , Often telling one simple narrative, based around one character. This is both by design, because direct statements are more easily remembered than florid ones, and by accident, as fables are passed from teller to teller, (ii)______ details fall away, leaving only the essential story.Fables often endure due to their (i)______ , Often telling one simple narrative, based around one character. This is both by design, because direct statements are more easily remembered than florid ones, and by accident, as fables are passed from teller to teller, (ii)______ details fall away, leaving only the essential story.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　simplicity; superfluous

The technology of integrated circuits inaugurated a new era of electronics, which can be characterized by saying that any electronic circuit, however (i) __________, that can be designed can also be made reliably and cheaply, provided it is manufactured and sold in sufficient numbers to (ii) __________ the cost of design and production.The technology of integrated circuits inaugurated a new era of electronics, which can be characterized by saying that any electronic circuit, however (i) __________, that can be designed can also be made reliably and cheaply, provided it is manufactured and sold in sufficient numbers to (ii) __________ the cost of design and production.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　complicated; recover
design
design: standard unit headword

But once the paradigm (ii)______ , the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more considered worthy publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted towards positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______ , the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more considered worthy publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine
affirm
affirm: standard unit headword

答案：D　aplomb

The only way to definitively test a large civil engineering structure such as a bridge is to build it in anticipation of possible challenges from nature and then let nature take its course. That is not to say that every new large structure is a (i) __________. Engineers understand a great deal about the behavior and limitations of their structures' components. Furthermore, it is the (ii) __________ structure that is built more than 10 or 20 percent larger than its predecessors, thus allowing engineers to move (iii) __________ into unknown territory.The only way to definitively test a large civil engineering structure such as a bridge is to build it in anticipation of possible challenges from nature and then let nature take its course. That is not to say that every new large structure is a (i) __________. Engineers understand a great deal about the behavior and limitations of their structures' components. Furthermore, it is the (ii) __________ structure that is built more than 10 or 20 percent larger than its predecessors, thus allowing engineers to move (iii) __________ into unknown territory.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　gamble; unusual; incrementally
nature

It turns out that lying is the more (ii)______ skill. #151 TC TRIPLE
Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.Why Zona Gale - a Pulitzer Prize-winning writer whose novels were both a critical and popular success - has fallen so far out of the literary canon is the fascinating subject of Deborah Lindsay Williams' recent book. Williams concludes that Gale's embrace of literary (i) __________ accounts for her eventual decline in reputation. Gale sought professional affiliation with other women writers of many stripes, and Williams contends that she has therefore been (ii) __________ by a later literary paradigm that favors (iii) __________ as a marker of artistic prowess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　sisterhood; marginalized; individual exceptionalism
subject
subject: standard unit headword
#152 SE
In early twentieth-century Peru, modernizing elites imagined the country’s mountain regions as teeming with indigenous peoples but thought of its Amazonian jungle as a vast, _______ expanse, a blank slate on which to inscribe modernity.In early twentieth-century Peru, modernizing elites imagined the country’s mountain regions as teeming with indigenous peoples but thought of its Amazonian jungle as a vast, _______ expanse, a blank slate on which to inscribe modernity.
答案：A, D　unpopulated; vacant

答案：D　variegated

nature: standard unit headword

The poem is not nearly as(i) as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (ii) by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.The poem is not nearly as(i) as it has often been said to be. Granted, it would be (ii) to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. But the poem is more self-contained than it appears when you read only fragments of it, and its allusions are often (ii) by their contexts or by their repeated appearances in changing contexts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　difficult; absurd; explained

This is a __________ book: it takes up and defends a position to which the current intellectual climate is generally hostile.This is a __________ book: it takes up and defends a position to which the current intellectual climate is generally hostile.
答案：B, E　plucky; courageous
subversive
subversive: standard unit headword

The author engages this issue from diverse perspectives, supports his arguments with many examples, and manages to avoid antagonizing others in dealing with a very ______ subject.The author engages this issue from diverse perspectives, supports his arguments with many examples, and manages to avoid antagonizing others in dealing with a very ______ subject.
答案：A, C　contentious; controversial

subject: standard unit headword

答案：C　derivative
nature
nature: standard unit headword
#158 TC TRIPLE
Uchiyama Shudian, specializing in Japanese texts, including translations, was the finest bookstore of its kind in Shanghai in the 1930s; its owner, Uchiyama Kanzo, envisioned himself as something of a missionary for the cause of Sino-Japanese cultural relations, bringing Chinese and Japanese writers together for positive dialogue. (i) __________, the (ii) __________ Uchiyama's shop remained (iii) __________: tensions between China and Japan in the 1930s made it impossible to continue the kind of interaction that had been achieved through Uchiyama's efforts.Uchiyama Shudian, specializing in Japanese texts, including translations, was the finest bookstore of its kind in Shanghai in the 1930s; its owner, Uchiyama Kanzo, envisioned himself as something of a missionary for the cause of Sino-Japanese cultural relations, bringing Chinese and Japanese writers together for positive dialogue. (i) __________, the (ii) __________ Uchiyama's shop remained (iii) __________: tensions between China and Japan in the 1930s made it impossible to continue the kind of interaction that had been achieved through Uchiyama's efforts.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　Regrettably; cultural exchange in; an anomaly

nature: standard unit headword
#159 Sentence Equivalence
Architects may be more extroverted and therefore the more ______ member of a bridge design team, but they are not always the most essential.Architects may be more extroverted and therefore the more ______ member of a bridge design team, but they are not always the most essential.
答案：D, F　visible; salient

design: standard unit headword
#160 TC SINGLE
In seventeenth-century science, we witness the first instance of technological __________: scientific tools, constructed from existing scientific knowledge, led to the acquisition of new knowledge that in turn contributed to improving the tools' design.In seventeenth-century science, we witness the first instance of technological __________: scientific tools, constructed from existing scientific knowledge, led to the acquisition of new knowledge that in turn contributed to improving the tools' design.
答案：B　feedback

design: standard unit headword

Indira Gandhi is independent India's most puzzling political figure. She was enigmatic and often (i) __________ in person, and her political identity was no less (ii) __________. As Prime Minister, she seemed to be a woman of great (iii) __________, but privately she was diffident and self-doubting.Indira Gandhi is independent India's most puzzling political figure. She was enigmatic and often (i) __________ in person, and her political identity was no less (ii) __________. As Prime Minister, she seemed to be a woman of great (iii) __________, but privately she was diffident and self-doubting.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　opaque; elusive; self-assurance

答案：A, B　singular; unique
reconstruct
reconstruct: standard unit headword
#163 TC SINGLE
Gravity is the __________ of the four fundamental forces existing in nature, the others being the electromagnetic force, and the strong and weak nuclear forces; yet over large distances it is gravity that dominates.Gravity is the __________ of the four fundamental forces existing in nature, the others being the electromagnetic force, and the strong and weak nuclear forces; yet over large distances it is gravity that dominates.
答案：D　least potent
nature
nature: standard unit headword
#164 TC TRIPLE
Letters present an awkward stumbling block to those who take a high line about literature as a custodian of moral values. The letters of accomplished poets and novelists are - as often as not - about drinking and infidelity, about sponging and wasting time. Consequently, one's (i) __________ the writer as (ii) __________ can often come in for a rude shock on contemplation of the writer's correspondence: the soul that composed the poems and the self that wrote the letters can seem (iii) __________.Letters present an awkward stumbling block to those who take a high line about literature as a custodian of moral values. The letters of accomplished poets and novelists are - as often as not - about drinking and infidelity, about sponging and wasting time. Consequently, one's (i) __________ the writer as (ii) __________ can often come in for a rude shock on contemplation of the writer's correspondence: the soul that composed the poems and the self that wrote the letters can seem (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　reverence for; exemplary; irreconcilable
idiosyncratic
idiosyncratic: standard unit headword
#165 Sentence Equivalence
As astronomers do not yet have a good understanding of the fundamental nature of dark matter, it should not be a surprise that astronomy students’ ideas about dark matter are, at best, ______.As astronomers do not yet have a good understanding of the fundamental nature of dark matter, it should not be a surprise that astronomy students’ ideas about dark matter are, at best, ______.
答案：A, B　superficial; sketchy
nature
nature: standard unit headword
#166 TC SINGLE
Readers are interested in Marx's life because of his ideas, but Sperber's book downplays his ideas to make room for his life, illustrating why intellectual biography can sometimes be a curiously __________ genre.Readers are interested in Marx's life because of his ideas, but Sperber's book downplays his ideas to make room for his life, illustrating why intellectual biography can sometimes be a curiously __________ genre.
答案：C　self-defeating
downplay
downplay: standard unit headword

Despite Goody's contention that a kind of literacy line cuts through history, dividing oral from print cultures, it seems that traditional tale-telling can (iii) __________ long after a print culture is established.Oral traditions are (i) __________ nearly everywhere, and in nonliterate societies they do not (ii) __________ at their first exposure to the written word. Despite Goody's contention that a kind of literacy line cuts through history, dividing oral from print cultures, it seems that traditional tale-telling can (iii) __________ long after a print culture is established.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　tenacious; collapse; flourish

答案：A, C　malfeasance; fraudulence

答案：E　measured

答案：B　immutable

答案：C, F　unremarkable; commonplace

答案：C, E　minimal; trifling

答案：A, B　a rejection of; a liberation from

Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)______ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Although viewed by many as (ii)______ the healing process, these factors may actually provide an important clue as to why placebo often work: they are part of an experience that can itself be a (iii)______ illness.The patients given a placebo in the course of clinical trials receive much more than a pharmacologically inert substance. Like the patients receiving the experimental drug, they (i)______ a thorough medical evaluation, a chance to discuss their condition with competent professionals, a diagnosis, and a treatment plan. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　get; incidental to; powerful antidote to

Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______.Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious

答案：C, F　rapacious; avaricious

#211 Text Completion
The nature of our recollections is (i)______. One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.The nature of our recollections is (i)______. One memory can seem (ii)______ whereas another must be coaxed out of our brain little by little. Although a moment that excites our emotions is more likely to be recorded than a (iii)______ experience, the sensory qualities of an event also play a part in how vividly and accurately we remember it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　inconsistent; immediate and colorful; routine
nature
nature: standard unit headword

答案：A, E　derision; ridicule

答案：D　disingenuous

答案：A　reliability

答案：A, E, I　a propitious; practical alternatives; persisted

#260 Text Completion
Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)______ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)______ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)______ .Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)______ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)______ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　interchangeable; loquacious; unkempt

meet: standard unit headword
#261 Text Completion
Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i)______. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii)______; our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii)______ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i)______. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii)______; our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii)______ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　discarded; immortality; death and decay
fad
fad: standard unit headword
#262 Text Completion
In this single volume, Kenny aims to survey for the general reader all of ancient philosophy. Understandably, space in such a book is (i)______ and he is not to be faulted for minor omissions. However, Kenny would have added significantly to his book’s value had he more effectively (ii)______ the influence of ancient philosophy on the subsequent tradition. As it is, newcomers to the subject will have little (iii)______ the afterlife enjoyed by ancient philosophy in the period 1600-1750.In this single volume, Kenny aims to survey for the general reader all of ancient philosophy. Understandably, space in such a book is (i)______ and he is not to be faulted for minor omissions. However, Kenny would have added significantly to his book’s value had he more effectively (ii)______ the influence of ancient philosophy on the subsequent tradition. As it is, newcomers to the subject will have little (iii)______ the afterlife enjoyed by ancient philosophy in the period 1600-1750.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　at a premium; signaled; sense of

Because the articles are in many senses the editors’ personal favorites, it is probably (i)______ to expect more from the introductions—but if the book is to be read by advanced students, such criticism would be as (ii) ______ as the articles themselves.The introductions to each section, written by the editors of the anthology, provide useful background material, but they do not provide critical analysis of the articles. Because the articles are in many senses the editors’ personal favorites, it is probably (i)______ to expect more from the introductions—but if the book is to be read by advanced students, such criticism would be as (ii) ______ as the articles themselves.

Blank 2
答案：C, F　unrealistic; informative

If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)______, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)______.Given our species’ increasing numbers and appetites which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change, even (i)______ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. #304 Text Completion
This book cannot be evaluated properly without examining the author’s choice of format, which is the (i)______ of the format of standard academic works; here the photographs take center stage, with the text playing only a supporting role. This layout poses many dangers for the serious historian, not the least of which being the (ii)______ reception that academics—motivated partly by (iii)______ but also by genuine concern over scholarly standards—generally reserve for books apparently aimed at the popular market.This book cannot be evaluated properly without examining the author’s choice of format, which is the (i)______ of the format of standard academic works; here the photographs take center stage, with the text playing only a supporting role. This layout poses many dangers for the serious historian, not the least of which being the (ii)______ reception that academics—motivated partly by (iii)______ but also by genuine concern over scholarly standards—generally reserve for books apparently aimed at the popular market.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　inverse; scornful; snobbish elitism
nature
nature: standard unit headword
#305 Text Completion
The documentation of Earth’s biodiversity is complicated by the (i)______ taxonomists. Those experts in classifying species tend to be (ii)______ North America and Europe, whereas most of the undocumented biodiversity is likely in the tropics.The documentation of Earth’s biodiversity is complicated by the (i)______ taxonomists. Those experts in classifying species tend to be (ii)______ North America and Europe, whereas most of the undocumented biodiversity is likely in the tropics.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　uneven distribution of; clustered in

#306 Text Completion
Though McDonough discusses (i)______ the filmmaker’s aesthetic principles, it is the description of the (ii)______ , the very vulgarity of the director’s films, rather than McDonough’s learned discourses on the aesthetics of the film, that makes the book so entertaining.Though McDonough discusses (i)______ the filmmaker’s aesthetic principles, it is the description of the (ii)______ , the very vulgarity of the director’s films, rather than McDonough’s learned discourses on the aesthetics of the film, that makes the book so entertaining.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　adroitly; flagrant crassness

#307 Text Completion
The inconspicuous location and lack of striking vistas that characterize the villa reflect the ______ aspect of its creator’s personality.The inconspicuous location and lack of striking vistas that characterize the villa reflect the ______ aspect of its creator’s personality.
答案：D　self-effacing

#308 Text Completion
The medical professor’s thesis—hardly new, but rarely ______ by a faculty member of his distinction—is that patients are more than the sum of the symptoms and systems.The medical professor’s thesis—hardly new, but rarely ______ by a faculty member of his distinction—is that patients are more than the sum of the symptoms and systems.
答案：A　espoused

#309 Text Completion
As theories, quantum field theory and theory of general relatively have been astonishingly (i)______ that no experiment yet has shown a crack in either of them, even though both of them (ii)______ wildly counterintuitive properties in physical reality. And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii)______ , a fact that has disallowed theorists—at least so far—from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.As theories, quantum field theory and theory of general relatively have been astonishingly (i)______ that no experiment yet has shown a crack in either of them, even though both of them (ii)______ wildly counterintuitive properties in physical reality. And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii)______ , a fact that has disallowed theorists—at least so far—from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　successful; posit; deeply at odds with each

Blank 2
答案：C, E　palliative; curative

#311 Text Completion
What she disliked in the fictions of some of her peers was the excessive (i)______. In her novel, by contrast, she was marked by a (ii)______ : she always (iii)______ .What she disliked in the fictions of some of her peers was the excessive (i)______. In her novel, by contrast, she was marked by a (ii)______ : she always (iii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　explicitness; reticence; keeps some details undisclosed

#312 Text Completion
Many legislators who helped Roosevelt shape the New Deal ______ the fact that emerging social problems affected every segment of the population; nonetheless, they often acted with a view to aiding only their own constituents.Many legislators who helped Roosevelt shape the New Deal ______ the fact that emerging social problems affected every segment of the population; nonetheless, they often acted with a view to aiding only their own constituents.
答案：B　bemoaned

#313 Text Completion
Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______, communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______, communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized

答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized
